title: SurePath Open Protocol (SPOP): A Resilient Interoperability Framework for Mitigating 'Cash Trap' Vulnerabilities in Critical Industrial Infrastructure
author: Thatikonda, Rahul Kumar
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Abstract  
   
 
 The United States Critical Industrial Infrastructure currently faces a dual crisis of digital fragmentation and financial fragility, phenomena inextricably linked through the mechanism of information latency. As manufacturing supply chains grow increasingly complex, the disconnect between Operational Technology (OT) and Information Technology (IT) creates profound visibility gaps. These gaps force defense contractors to hold excessive safety stock to buffer against uncertainty—a condition financial covenants term a "Cash Trap". This paper presents the "SurePath Open Protocol (SPOP)", a novel interoperability profile designed to bridge legacy silos through a governed Unified Namespace (UNS). SPOP harmonizes OPC UA (Southbound) and MQTT Sparkplug B (Northbound) within a Zero Trust architecture compliant with NIST SP 800-207 and CMMC Level 2. Unlike standard implementations, SPOP enforces payload integrity via ECDSA (secp256r1) signatures and SHA-256 hash-chaining, ensuring that financial capital is only released upon cryptographically verified physical events. We evaluate SPOP using a simpy-based discrete event simulation subjected to "Chaos Monkey" resilience testing ( iterations). Results demonstrate a 94.5% reduction in P99 latency (dropping from 1,245ms in legacy polling architectures to 68ms) and the successful autonomous blocking of unauthorized control commands using Isolation Forest anomaly detection. This framework provides the real-time, context-aware serialization necessary to reduce required working capital by approximately 22%, effectively securing the DIB against both cyber and financial volatility. 
  The reference implementation and simulation code for this project are available at:  https://github.com/trahulkumar/SPOP-Reference-Architecture
steps_raw:
Abstract  
   
 
 The United States Critical Industrial Infrastructure currently faces a dual crisis of digital fragmentation and financial fragility, phenomena inextricably linked through the mechanism of information latency. As manufacturing supply chains grow increasingly complex, the disconnect between Operational Technology (OT) and Information Technology (IT) creates profound visibility gaps. These gaps force defense contractors to hold excessive safety stock to buffer against uncertainty—a condition financial covenants term a "Cash Trap". This paper presents the "SurePath Open Protocol (SPOP)", a novel interoperability profile designed to bridge legacy silos through a governed Unified Namespace (UNS). SPOP harmonizes OPC UA (Southbound) and MQTT Sparkplug B (Northbound) within a Zero Trust architecture compliant with NIST SP 800-207 and CMMC Level 2. Unlike standard implementations, SPOP enforces payload integrity via ECDSA (secp256r1) signatures and SHA-256 hash-chaining, ensuring that financial capital is only released upon cryptographically verified physical events. We evaluate SPOP using a simpy-based discrete event simulation subjected to "Chaos Monkey" resilience testing ( iterations). Results demonstrate a 94.5% reduction in P99 latency (dropping from 1,245ms in legacy polling architectures to 68ms) and the successful autonomous blocking of unauthorized control commands using Isolation Forest anomaly detection. This framework provides the real-time, context-aware serialization necessary to reduce required working capital by approximately 22%, effectively securing the DIB against both cyber and financial volatility. 
  The reference implementation and simulation code for this project are available at:  https://github.com/trahulkumar/SPOP-Reference-Architecture
